subUnits.title: 3.1 Climate of FCT: Temperature, Rainfall, Humidity
notes: FCT has a tropical savanna climate (Aw according to Köppen classification) with distinct wet and dry seasons.
• Wet season: April to October (7 months)
• Dry season: November to March (5 months)
• Southern parts receive more rainfall than northern parts
• August break: Short dry period in August
• High humidity makes wet season feel hotter
• Wind speed: Generally light to moderate
• Harmattan winds bring dust from Sahara
• High evaporation rates in dry season
• Moderate climate compared to coastal areas
• Less humid than Lagos and southern Nigeria

quiz.q: The climate of FCT Abuja is classified as:
quiz.explanation: FCT has a tropical savanna climate (Aw) with distinct wet and dry seasons.

quiz.q: The annual average temperature in FCT is:
quiz.explanation: FCT has an annual average temperature of 25-30°C, making it moderately warm.

quiz.q: The wet season in FCT lasts from:
quiz.explanation: The wet season in FCT runs from April to October (approximately 7 months).

quiz.q: Annual rainfall in FCT ranges between:
quiz.explanation: FCT receives between 1,100-1,500mm of rainfall annually, concentrated in the wet season.

quiz.q: The hottest period in FCT is:
quiz.explanation: March-April is the hottest period in FCT, with temperatures reaching 35-40°C.

quiz.q: Relative humidity during the wet season in FCT is:
quiz.explanation: During the wet season, relative humidity in FCT is high, ranging from 70-90%.

quiz.q: The Harmattan season in FCT occurs during:
quiz.explanation: Harmattan (dry, dust-laden wind) occurs from November to March in FCT.

quiz.q: Peak rainfall in FCT occurs in:
quiz.explanation: The peak rainfall period in FCT is July-September, when rainfall is heaviest.

quiz.q: The wind that brings rainfall to FCT is:
quiz.explanation: The Southwest monsoon wind from the Atlantic Ocean brings moisture and rainfall to FCT during the wet season.

quiz.q: Annual sunshine hours in FCT range from:
quiz.explanation: FCT receives approximately 2,500-3,000 hours of sunshine annually, with more in the dry season.

subUnits.title: 3.2 Vegetation Type and Population of FCT
notes: FCT falls within the Guinea Savannah vegetation zone with the following characteristics:
• Trees shed leaves during dry season
• Grasses dry up in dry season
• Frequent bush fires in dry season
• Deep root systems to reach water table
• Grasses grow rapidly in wet season
• Vegetation becomes sparse in dry season
• Crops: maize, sorghum, millet, yam, cassava
• One of the fastest growing cities in Africa
• Lower density in rural area councils
• Indigenous groups: Gbagyi, Gade, Koro, Nupe
• Migrants from all parts of Nigeria
• Mix of formal and informal sector workers

quiz.q: The natural vegetation of FCT is:
quiz.explanation: FCT falls within the Guinea Savannah zone, characterized by tall grasses and scattered trees.

quiz.q: Common trees in FCT's vegetation include:
quiz.explanation: Common trees include Isoberlinia doka, Daniellia oliveri, Parkia biglobosa, and other savannah species.

quiz.q: The population of FCT according to 2006 census was:
quiz.explanation: The 2006 census recorded FCT's population as 1,406,239, though current estimates exceed 3 million.

quiz.q: Trees in the Guinea Savannah have thick bark to:
quiz.explanation: Thick bark helps trees resist frequent bush fires that occur during the dry season in savannah regions.

quiz.q: The indigenous people of FCT include:
quiz.explanation: Indigenous groups in FCT include Gbagyi, Gade, Koro, and Nupe, though the population is now very diverse.

quiz.q: Grasses in FCT's vegetation are typically:
quiz.explanation: Savannah grasses in FCT typically grow 1-3 meters high during the wet season.

quiz.q: The fastest growing area in FCT is:
quiz.explanation: AMAC (Abuja Municipal Area Council) has the highest population density and fastest growth rate.

quiz.q: Trees in FCT shed their leaves during:
quiz.explanation: Trees in the savannah shed leaves during the dry season to conserve water.

quiz.q: Major factors driving population growth in FCT include:
quiz.explanation: Population growth is driven by both natural increase (births exceeding deaths) and rural-urban migration.

quiz.q: A major challenge of rapid population growth in FCT is:
quiz.explanation: Rapid population growth creates challenges including housing deficit, traffic congestion, and pressure on infrastructure.
